answers.answer: D) Pre-aggregate to a user_daily table (user_id, date) with a unique index, then use the LAG/window approach on the much smaller aggregated table (~50M rows). Reason: This is the only scalable solution. The key insight: 500M events aggregate to ~50M user-day combinations (500M / 10 events per user-day avg = 50M unique user-days). Pre-aggregation reduces data by 10x, then window function runs on manageable dataset. Option A (recursive CTE) materializes 90 * N_users rows - explodes. Option B sorts 500M rows - too slow. Option C pulls all into memory - OOM.
